column1: # oraclemcp conformance clause manifest — the machine-readable source of truth for
# tests/conformance/COVERAGE.md. Regenerate the doc with scripts/gen_coverage_report.sh.
# Columns (TAB-separated): id  level(MUST|SHOULD)  section  tested(y|n)  verdict(pass|fail|xfail)  disc(DISC-nnn|-)  description
# An xfail verdict MUST reference a DISCREPANCIES.md id; a fail verdict drops MUST coverage below 1.00.

column7: `initialize` returns protocol version, server info, and tool capability.
`notifications/initialized` produces no response.
`tools/list` returns MCP `inputSchema` objects.
`tools/list` returns non-empty titles and explicit `readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `idempotentHint`, and `openWorldHint` annotations.
`tools/list` preserves declared `outputSchema`, and `tools/call` `structuredContent` validates against the advertised schema.
`tools/call` returns `content`, `structuredContent`, and `isError`.
Unknown tools are MCP tool errors, not transport crashes.
Initialize capabilities advertise resources only after resource handlers are served.
Served capabilities degrade to the negotiated revision: `completions` (added 2025-03-26) is not advertised to a 2024-11-05 client; refusal fields (`isError` + content text) are revision-independent.
A session initializes exactly once: a second `initialize` is rejected with a JSON-RPC error and keeps the originally negotiated version.
`resources/list`, `resources/templates/list`, and `resources/read` are served with MCP resource content objects.
`prompts/list` and `prompts/get` are served only after prompt capability negotiation.
Malformed JSON returns parse error with null id.
Unknown methods return method-not-found and echo id.
Invalid params return invalid-params and echo id.
Oversized frames fail closed before parsing.
Batch arrays are explicitly rejected for stdio.
Token mismatch errors do not echo the presented token.
`tools/list`, `resources/list`, and `resources/templates/list` emit an opaque, tamper-evident `nextCursor` that round-trips to cover every item exactly once within a bounded page size.
A forged, edited, or cross-endpoint pagination cursor (and a tampered `oracle_query` page cursor) is rejected with invalid-params / a structured error envelope, never silently followed.

A large `oracle_query` result is materialized as an `oracle-export://{id}` resource (E3) and returned as a `resource_link` (E3b); `resources/read` serves it with its MIME type and proper CSV escaping; a forged or expired export id fails closed (`OBJECT_NOT_FOUND`).

An export is access-controlled identically to the originating query: the export id is bound (HMAC) to the request's OAuth scope-grant fingerprint, so a `resources/read` under a different scope grant is refused.

`resources.subscribe` is advertised, and `resources/subscribe` accepted, ONLY when a change source is confirmed (the `SubscriptionHub` polling fallback). With no source the capability stays `false` and `resources/subscribe` is refused (method-not-found). The thin-line binary ships with no source, so it does not advertise subscribe.

A subscribed resource whose polled fingerprint changes (the DBMS_CHANGE_NOTIFICATION-vs-polling fallback path) emits a `notifications/resources/updated` for that uri on the next transport flush; the first observation only seeds the baseline.

`completion/complete` is advertised (`capabilities.completions`) and served as owner→type→object autocomplete for the dictionary tools' arguments and the `oracle://object/{owner}/{type}/{name}` template, scoped by `context.arguments`, routed through the read path, capped at 100 values with `{values,total,hasMore}`. A `profile`/`db` argument completes only the E5 `mcp_exposed` profiles (a non-exposed profile is never offered).

A `tools/call` carrying `params._meta.progressToken` is bracketed by `notifications/progress` (a started 0/1 and a completed 1/1) that ride the transport after the response and are true notifications (no id); without a token no progress is emitted.

`notifications/tools/list_changed` is advertised (`tools.listChanged: true`) and emitted when the served tool set changes (e.g. an `oracle_switch_profile` that alters the profile-scoped custom-tool catalog); it is a paramless, id-less notification.
OAuth-protected `/mcp` refuses anonymous requests with `WWW-Authenticate`.
A valid bearer token admits a served HTTP request and forwards the validated OAuth scope grant to tool dispatch.
A valid bearer token without the configured required scope returns `403` with `error="insufficient_scope"`.
Narrow, broad, and profile-protected OAuth scope ceilings are enforced at dispatch through the binary HTTP transport.
Service-owned per-client MCP bearer credentials are hashed at rest, authenticate as scoped isolated principals, and rotate/revoke one client without affecting another.
A disallowed browser `Origin` is rejected with `403` before MCP dispatch.
Stateful HTTP rejects forged or unknown `mcp-session-id` values before MCP dispatch.
Stateful GET replays buffered SSE responses after `cursor` / `Last-Event-ID`; stateless DELETE returns 405 instead of a false session-close acceptance.
`/operator/v1` API routes return typed JSON 404 responses and preserve parsed query filters; they never fall through to a SPA/history HTML response.
`/mcp` POST rejects unacceptable `Accept` and unsupported `Content-Type` values before JSON-RPC dispatch.
`/mcp` honors `MCP-Protocol-Version`; unsupported versions return typed JSON `400 unsupported_protocol_version` before dispatch.
`/operator/v1/schema` serves the generated machine-readable schema bundle with route and event schemas.
`/operator/v1/health`, `/metrics`, `/audit-tail`, `/active-lanes`, and `/vsession` return versioned, redacted REST envelopes with unavailable-source degradation where providers are absent.
`/operator/v1/events` emits SSE `operatorEvent` envelopes carrying `event_seq`, `event_id`, `lane_id`, `subject_id_hash`, `redaction_level`, and `schema_version`.
Gated-action operator routes forward to existing MCP `tools/call` dispatch mappings rather than bypassing the guarded dispatcher.
Generated operator TypeScript types and UI fixtures are checked against the Rust schema source of truth.
B.6 `mcp_and_operator_v1_conformance_matrix` records 1.00 MUST coverage and runs the UI fixture schema validator.

Gated-action operator routes accept or derive idempotency keys, return typed in-progress/conflict responses, and replay the original redacted response for same-key retries without re-entering guarded dispatch.
`/operator/v1/events` resumes by `cursor` / `Last-Event-ID` from a bounded subject+lane ring, emits gap markers or typed expiry for stale cursors, and rejects cross-lane cursors before replay.
B.8 change proposal apply re-classifies stored SQL templates and re-checks level/grants/subject at apply time instead of trusting stored proposal verdicts.
The embedded dashboard bundle serves the SPA when enabled, never falls back from API routes to HTML, and the bundle gate checks npm audit, lockfile provenance, SBOM shape, and content hashes.

A malicious page cannot trigger dashboard gated actions: browser POSTs require same-origin, HttpOnly/SameSite cookie, CSRF, route action ticket, CSP frame controls, and no browser-storage token persistence.
Config draft/apply is staged, strictly validated, redacted, atomic, audited, reload-aware, and rollback restores the prior config without serializing secret references.
Workbench execution has no alternate SQL path: preview/read/write route through the same classifier, ceilings, preview/confirm, idempotency, audit, and dispatcher path used by MCP agents.
Change proposal apply re-classifies every current SQL template, re-checks level/grants/subject at apply time, forwards through gated actions, and treats stored verdicts as review metadata only.
Dashboard skin grammar is a contract: 2D and table no-WebGL fallbacks stay available, Orrery remains lazy/quarantined, a11y anchors stay present, and credential secrets are never rendered.

Explorer global search spans all visible schemas and object types for object-name matches and source text matches through the existing guarded `oracle_search_objects` and `oracle_search_source` action routes.

Audit proof-bundle export is allow-list-first: dashboard requests `export=proof-bundle`, the operator response includes subject hashes, SQL hashes, DB evidence, chain/signature metadata, and no raw SQL, bind values, raw subject ids, or secrets.
Client-credential screen lists only redacted per-client metadata, shows a rotated bearer once, and revoke/rotate closes only the affected principal's sessions/grants.

Source-replaceable governed DDL captures prior source into content-addressed service snapshots when visible, source-history lists omit source text, and revert drafts a reviewed DDL Change Proposal instead of directly applying DDL.

The surface inventory asserts per-surface authn/gating for `/mcp`, stateful SSE GET, `/operator/v1`, dashboard pairing/POSTs, config apply, `/readyz`, and `/metrics`; unauthenticated infra probes expose no DB identity, SQL text, bind values, wallet, credential, or password markers.
Server-only native TLS accepts a valid HTTPS handshake.
Native mTLS rejects clients without a certificate and accepts a client certificate signed by the configured CA.
A published JSON Schema exists for `OracleCell::structured` and declares ARRAY, JSON/OSON, VECTOR, TSTZ, object marker, and generic unsupported variants.
Golden fixtures for ARRAY/JSON/VECTOR/TSTZ, OSON scalars, object/UDT unsupported, and generic unsupported parse and match the serializer output.
Structured payloads serialize verbatim rather than flattening through text.
Legacy silent-flattening shapes are rejected by the schema contract test.
`OracleCell::structured` carries the structured contract version, the published schema declares it, and metadata cache keys include it.
Structured ARRAY/JSON/VECTOR decode is capped by row, cell, byte, and depth budgets; larger query budgets require `deep_decode=true`.

Committing tools write a durable pre-execute intent, unresolved in-doubt intents fail writable startup closed, and recovered terminal history rejects exact confirmation-grant plus SQL replay after restart.
Service backup snapshots the XDG state directory plus config under the service lock, restore verifies the backed-up audit hash-chain before recovering files, and audit data remains non-prunable.
Mimalloc is wired as an explicit opt-in allocator feature, background worker creation is gated by service/runtime admission, and the DL-4 lock hierarchy is documented and debug-asserted.

`audit verify --with-db-evidence` verifies the signed chain, correlates audit sequence numbers with signed Oracle session evidence when present, and reports degraded evidence without exposing SQL, bind values, or secrets.
Per-session and per-subject lanes keep operating level, profile, connection, and session state isolated.
Confirmation and execution grants are single-use and bound to lane, subject, session, profile, and generation.
Different configured databases stay isolated under concurrent live lanes.
Blocked or contended database work cannot head-of-line-block unrelated lanes and must finish or return a typed timeout/deadlock/busy result.
Capacity is bounded, redacted, reserve-aware, and fair across subjects.
DELETE, timeout, cancel, shutdown, and reaper terminal paths release permits and roll back dirty work exactly once.
Streamable HTTP lanes coexist with the frozen stdio contract without changing stdio golden behavior.
Concurrent actions keep per-subject audit identity, valid hash chains, and idempotency replay semantics.
Mixed lane live/load evidence captures latency percentiles and zero leak/starvation verdicts.
SSE ids, Last-Event-ID/cursor replay, typed expiry/gaps, and DELETE are scoped to the target session/stream.
The lane model forbids permit leaks, stale grants, ceiling races, and subject/connection/audit mixing.
